description: Reusable validation rules for Studio registries, DataByteSprites indexes, Phase 6.0 runtime manifests, progression ownership, source ownership maps, diagnostics alignment, documentation drift checks, and mechanics graphs.

validationEngine.goal: Validate the single module bootstrap, deterministic battle core, canonical progression and player-save owners, screen and presentation owners, cross-index consistency, documentation drift, and diagnostics readiness.

ruleSets.name: Registry Availability
ruleSets.description: Every registered Knowledge Engine or diagnostics source must load, parse, declare a schemaVersion when JSON-backed, and expose at least one expected collection record.

ruleSets.name: Required Fields
ruleSets.description: Records must expose stable IDs and readable titles so they can participate in Studio search, graph validation, diagnostics, and future tooling. Mechanics graph edges may use synthetic IDs generated from source, target, and edge type.

ruleSets.name: Cross-Index References
ruleSets.description: Dependencies, relationships, learnedBy fields, assignedTo fields, dex refs, and graph edges should point to known Studio or DataByteSprites IDs. Canon story and system references may be registered as known global IDs until their full records are added.

ruleSets.name: Mechanics Graph Integrity
ruleSets.description: Every mechanics graph edge must reference existing graph nodes and use a declared edge type.

ruleSets.name: Runtime Load Order
ruleSets.description: Runtime modules must have unique IDs, valid dependencies, and reachable scripts before the runtime can be trusted.

ruleSets.name: Canonical Runtime Ownership
ruleSets.description: Data Discovery diagnostics must recognize the module bootstrap as loader, Product App v4 as orchestrator, the screen registry as screen dispatcher, app presentation as layout owner, and split runtimes as gameplay owners. Archived compatibility layers must not own HP, Signal, download odds, battle state, screens, or layout.

ruleSets.name: Duplicate Gameplay Ownership
ruleSets.description: Gameplay systems should have one active owner. Legacy files may remain indexed as references, but diagnostics should warn if they are marked as active gameplay owners.

ruleSets.name: Product App UI Regression
ruleSets.description: The canonical mobile battle UI uses HP rings for both lead and wild sprites while Signal is rendered separately as encounter stability. Horizontal battle HP bars are deprecated in the main battle layout.

ruleSets.name: Orphan Detection
ruleSets.description: Useful records should either be referenced by another system or clearly marked as planned, design-seed, active-foundation, legacy-reference, compatibility, or archive content.

ruleSets.name: Documentation Drift
ruleSets.description: README and roadmap documentation should reflect the active Phase 6.0 module bootstrap, battle core, progression loop, Product App v4 shell, canonical ownership, and diagnostics capabilities.

gameplayOwnership.battleEvents: none; battle core returns one structured result

uiCanon.hpDisplay: thin outer HP progress edges for lead and wild sprites
uiCanon.signalDisplay: Separate Signal meter for encounter stability

canonGlobalIds.label: Vic
canonGlobalIds.description: Primary DataByteSprites protagonist referenced by lore records. A full character record should replace this global ID later.

canonGlobalIds.label: First Capture Record
canonGlobalIds.description: Canon DataByteDex reference attached to Leovolt and the first capture event. A full DataByteDex record should replace this global ID later.

canonGlobalIds.label: DataByteSprites Lore Core
canonGlobalIds.description: Foundational lore system referenced by species and story records until a dedicated lore-core index is created.

canonGlobalIds.label: Admin Console
canonGlobalIds.description: Future Admin-facing profile, scanner authority, unlock, and progression system referenced by Dennis and NullKing records.

canonGlobalIds.label: Rarity Effects
canonGlobalIds.description: Future visual and mechanical rarity effect layer referenced by screen and signal manifestation rules.

nextImplementationSteps: Keep the Diagnostics source registry as the single list of validation targets.
Teach the validation engine to consume canonicalRuntime rules from this manifest.
Validate Product App v4, module bootstrap, screen registry, presentation runtime, and split runtime owners against the runtime manifest and source-files index.
Warn when legacy-reference files are marked as active gameplay owners.
Validate HP ring and Signal meter UI expectations through runtime health checks.
Run diagnostics and compare health, coverage, errors, warnings, and cross-index consistency after Phase 4.9 alignment.
